subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, TI) + non-Bahraini press; Bahraini state media EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF validation.

A Sunni monarchy ruling a Shia-majority population (the 2011 uprising was crushed with Gulf help); the dinar is hard-pegged to the USD. Migrant workers are roughly half the population under kafala-legacy controls.

Standout drags: the weakest fiscal position in the Gulf, mass post-2011 political imprisonment, and a sectarian fat-tail; offsets are a strong finance sector, the US 5th-Fleet alliance, and low crime.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), a fragile/violated 8 Apr ceasefire, and the global energy shock. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A strong finance sector and peg-anchored low inflation, against the Gulf's weakest fiscal position.

fiscal_state.notes: The weakest Gulf fiscal position - debt ~120%+ GDP, persistent deficits, depleting oil, reliant on GCC support after the 2018 bailout. (IMF Art IV; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: A hard, long-standing USD peg - near-zero autonomy but stable. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Peg stability but thin reserve buffers; not a reserve issuer. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A large, well-regulated financial-services hub - a relative strength, with a sovereign-bank-nexus vulnerability. (IMF; CBB)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Oil exports and refining feedstock move via the blockaded strait; a small, highly exposed economy. (Reuters)

institutional.notes: Capable wealth-funded administration, against no political-sphere judicial independence and a crushed press.

rule_of_law.notes: A near-absolute monarchy with no judicial independence on political matters and corruption. (Freedom House; V-Dem; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF-validated; the political sphere is opaque. (IMF)

press_freedom.notes: A bottom-tier RSF standing, with independent media crushed post-2011. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Suppressed stability over a sectarian majority, with mass political imprisonment.

political_stability.notes: A Sunni monarchy over a Shia majority; the 2011 uprising was crushed with Gulf help - suppressed stability with a sectarian fat-tail. (Freedom House; HRW)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'Not Free' - mass political imprisonment, the opposition dissolved, and criminalised expression. (Freedom House; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Easy employer-sponsored entry and no income tax, but a kafala-legacy ceiling, no citizenship path, and a politicised-naturalisation grievance. (HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: A deep, structural Sunni-Shia sectarian cleavage. (HRW; V-Dem)

demographics.notes: Migrant-skewed (~half the population) with a small young citizen base. (UN; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A US-anchored Gulf ally with an open account, against Iran-proximity exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: Hosts the US Navy 5th Fleet, a major non-NATO ally, Gulf-backed, and normalised with Israel. (CRS; Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: Home of the US Navy Fifth Fleet and a primary Iranian retaliation target in the 2026 war; sectarian-unrest risk amplified. (Al Jazeera)

physical_practical.notes: Modern healthcare, infrastructure, and low crime, against extreme heat and sea-level risk.

climate.notes: A tiny low-lying Gulf state - extreme wet-bulb heat by mid-century and sea-level/coastal squeeze, partly offset by wealth/AC. (IPCC AR6; WB)

infrastructure.notes: A desalination plant on this water-scarce island was actually struck during the war — a direct hit to the drinking-water lifeline, not just a threat. (NYT; Guardian)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low natural-catastrophe exposure besides heat and sea-level. (UN)

summary: A small Gulf monarchy with a strong financial-services sector, peg-anchored low inflation, modern healthcare and infrastructure, low crime, and a US-anchored alliance (the 5th Fleet). Those are offset by the weakest fiscal position in the Gulf (debt ~120%+ of GDP, reliant on GCC support), and - more structurally - a Sunni monarchy ruling a Shia-majority population whose 2011 uprising was crushed, leaving mass political imprisonment, a dissolved opposition, a near-worst press environment, and a sectarian fat-tail. A USD peg trades monetary autonomy for stability.
